description: JTI additionalInfoTags registry. Derived from catalog §4 evidence (4 tags V0/V1 baseline-observed) + §4.5 unverified hypotheses (5 tags V3). Tags live under <ns9:additionalInfoTags> wrappers with two required children: <ns9:tagType>, <ns9:tagValue>. Multiple tags on the same scope are emitted as SIBLING <additionalInfoTags> wrappers (not multiple children under one wrapper).

children.tagValue: string (content — value semantic varies per tagType per this registry)

wireStructure.repeatability: Multiple sibling <ns9:additionalInfoTags> wrappers are allowed inside a single <ns9:idReferences>. Each sibling has exactly one tagType + one tagValue child. Builder MUST emit repeated top-level wrappers; parser MUST iterate all <additionalInfoTags> elements, not just the first.
scopeLocations: <ns9:idReferences>/<ns9:additionalInfoTags> — party-level tags scoped to a referenced entity

valueSemanticKinds.digit-boolean: String content MUST be literal '0' (false) or '1' (true). NOT XML-literal 'true'/'false'. Distinct from classType='boolean' which uses XML-literal form.
valueSemanticKinds.string-enum: String content matches one of a fixed enum. Parser MUST fail-closed on unknown tokens; builder MUST emit only declared enum values.
valueSemanticKinds.free-text: Arbitrary string content (e.g., email address, identifier).
valueSemanticKinds.email: RFC 5322-compatible email address as string content. UI validates; wire treats as free-text.

E_SERVICE.label: Consent to Electronic Service
E_SERVICE.description: Party opt-in/opt-out of eService. DUAL ENCODING: for existing-data parties uses this tag; for new-data parties the same semantic is expressed as <ns10:eService>true</ns10:eService> inline under <caseParticipantValue> (XML-literal form).

pairedWith.condition: When tagValue='1' (opt-in), EFSP_EMAIL is typically emitted as a sibling tag with the delivery address. Both tags live inside the SAME <idReferences> block.

openQuestions: Does JTI tolerate XML-literal 'true'/'false' tagValues? Policy: emit digit form; accept either form on parse; log warning if literal form appears.
Tag presence when party has no eService opinion — omit or emit '0'? Samples show present for every party with any preference.

FEE_EXEMPTION.label: Fee Exemption / Waiver Indicator
FEE_EXEMPTION.description: Party-level fee exemption. Audit C-3 fix: single tagType with enum tagValue, NOT a checkbox. Observed values: GOVT_ENTITY (government entity exempt), FEE_WAIVER (fee waiver granted/requested).
relatedClassTypes: contact — CIV-SUB-003 pairs FEE_WAIVER with a companion contact classType (FILING_PARTY_ADDRESS code) for the waiver-requesting party's address
auditFindings: Audit C-3 (tag-semantics inversion): schema side — type MUST be dropdown with {GOVT_ENTITY, FEE_WAIVER} enum, NOT checkbox. Pre-fix `JtiFieldSchema.json` had type=checkbox.
openQuestions: Full enum domain: only GOVT_ENTITY and FEE_WAIVER observed. Possibly PRIOR_PAID, DEFERRED, INDIGENT? Check JTI HTML + Madera live.
Relationship to declared-unverified EFSP_GOVERNMENT_EXEMPT / EFSP_FEE_WAIVER_FILED (§4.5) — duplicates or distinct tagTypes?

EFSP_FIRST_APPEARANCE_PAID.label: First Appearance Fee Already Paid (Self-Certification)
EFSP_FIRST_APPEARANCE_PAID.description: Filer self-certifies that the first-appearance fee for this party has been paid in a prior filing and should not be charged again. Self-certification — no JTI cross-check observed.
openQuestions: Is '0' ever emitted, or is tag omitted when not paid? Only '1' observed — policy-driven (EFSP may only include tag when self-certifying yes).
Validity scope: self-certified by filer with no backing verification, or JTI cross-checks prior filings?

EFSP_EMAIL.label: EFSP Delivery Email for eService
EFSP_EMAIL.description: Email address for eService delivery. Always paired with E_SERVICE=1 as a sibling tag in the same <idReferences> block. NOT a boolean; NOT an enum.

pairedWith.condition: REQUIRED-WITH-E_SERVICE=1. Never observed standalone; never paired with E_SERVICE=0.

auditFindings: Schema gap pre-T-3a: EFSP_EMAIL was missing entirely from `JtiFieldSchema.json` (§4.4) despite being evidence-backed. Added in this T-3a S2 deliverable.
openQuestions: Email format validation on JTI side — strict RFC 5322 or permissive?
Multiple EFSP_EMAIL sibling tags per party (alternate delivery addresses)? No baseline evidence either way.

EFSP_GOVERNMENT_EXEMPT.label: Government Entity (Legacy/Unverified)
EFSP_GOVERNMENT_EXEMPT.description: UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS. Declared in legacy `JtiFieldSchema.json` as checkbox. No baseline evidence in 48 canonical samples. May be a duplicate of FEE_EXEMPTION=GOVT_ENTITY.
EFSP_GOVERNMENT_EXEMPT.failClosedPolicy: Builder MUST NOT emit. Parser emits warning log on encounter, proceeds with best-effort mapping to observe live exposure empirically.
evidencePaths: (a) Check Madera GetPolicy for whether both this tagType and FEE_EXEMPTION appear.
(b) Search JTI HTML docs + per-court variant sample XMLs.
(c) If Madera only declares FEE_EXEMPTION, delete this entry in a future T-3a maintenance pass.

EFSP_FEE_WAIVER_FILED.label: Fee Waiver Filed (Legacy/Unverified)
EFSP_FEE_WAIVER_FILED.description: UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS. Declared in legacy schema as checkbox. No baseline evidence. May be a status tag (distinct from the request tag FEE_EXEMPTION=FEE_WAIVER) or a reply-path-only tag.
EFSP_FEE_WAIVER_FILED.failClosedPolicy: Builder MUST NOT emit. Parser emits warning log on encounter.
evidencePaths: (a) Determine if status tag vs request tag.
(c) Consider whether this is a reply-from-court tag the EFSP receives but never sends — if so, response-parser path only, not request-builder.

SELF_REPRESENTED.label: Self-Representation (Hypothesis)
SELF_REPRESENTED.description: UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS. NOT currently in legacy schema. Self-representation is currently modeled via ABSENT caseAssignment reference OR classType='boolean' with code=SELF_REP (see §3.7), NOT via a dedicated tag. Included in this registry only as evidence-gathering breadcrumb.
SELF_REPRESENTED.failClosedPolicy: Builder MUST NOT emit. Parser emits warning log on encounter. If Madera live declares SELF_REPRESENTED as a distinct tag, promote to V1 and update builder/parser.
evidencePaths: (a) CIV-SUB-008, CIV-SUB-018 do NOT use this tag — they model self-rep via absent caseAssignment.
(b) Unless Madera live declares SELF_REPRESENTED as separate, retain as unverified.

SEALED.label: Sealed Filing (Hypothesis)
SEALED.description: UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS. NOT in current schema. Madera policy ExtraFlags has supportsConditionalSeal=true — hypothesis is that Madera may expose a SEALED tag when a filing is conditionally sealed.

evidence.layerAEvidence: Madera policy ExtraFlags.supportsConditionalSeal=true (indirect — declares the capability but does not name the tag)

SEALED.failClosedPolicy: Builder MUST NOT emit. Parser emits warning log on encounter.
evidencePaths: (a) No sealed filings in baseline samples.
(b) Madera policy check — does it expose 'SEALED' as a tagType string?
(c) If Madera declares, add to schema + document in catalog §4.6.

ERRONEOUSLY_SUED_TRUENAME.label: Erroneously Sued True Name (Hypothesis)
ERRONEOUSLY_SUED_TRUENAME.description: UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS. NOT in current schema. Prior audit §5.2 referenced as free-text. No baseline evidence. Possibly LASC-specific.
ERRONEOUSLY_SUED_TRUENAME.failClosedPolicy: Builder MUST NOT emit. Parser emits warning log on encounter.
evidencePaths: (a) Check JTI HTML docs for LASC-specific usage.
(b) If truly free-text, model as tagValueKind='free-text' with uiType='text'.
